channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.thesixthaxis.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.thesixthaxis.com

item.title: 007 First Light races to 1.5 million sales in first 24 hours
item.content:encoded:
IO Interactive has celebrated a successful launch for 007 First Light, their long-awaited James Bond game, announcing that over 1.5 million copies were sold within the game’s first 24 hours.

“1.5 million copies sold!” IO posted on social media. “Thank you for showing up to the launch of 007 First Light, for your overwhelming enthusiasm and for sharing your experience with the game.”

It makes for great news for the Hitman developer, who were branching out into their first licensed game after spending the bulk of the 2010s working on their Hitman: World of Assassination trilogy. While, on the surface level, Agent 47’s exploits and subterfuge made IO a perfect fit for a James Bond game, with the character relying on similar traits to infiltrate locations, the end result is a game with much more of an action adventure feel, calling back to the likes of Uncharted, the hacking of Watch Dogs, and then with out-and-out firefights and car chase sequences.

We’ve not quite pinned a score on our review, but Jim has said “it’s an incredibly well-executed action adventure with a truly engrossing story, slick presentation, and layered gameplay systems.”

Source: IO Interactive



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/05/29/007-first-light-races-to-1-5-million-sales-in-first-24-hours/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: An Edge of Memories demo is coming to Steam in mid-June
item.content:encoded:
Midgar Studio and publisher Nacon have announced that a demo for Edge of Memories is coming to Steam on 15th June, with the French-made JRPG set to launch for PS5, Xbox Series X|S and PC later this year.

The demo will preview the game’s opening, offering around two hours of content form the game’s first chapter, featuring the Estfalian Coast region and a boss encounter.

It will pick up as protagonist Eline wakes up on the Estfalian Coast and meets the mysterious Ysoris. Accompanying Ysoris to the nearest town, they’re forced to team up and fight monsters come from the Corrosion to rescue local hunters. Being right at the start of the game, Eline’s still learning her first combos, Ysoris’ abilities are just emerging for you, and you’ve got an enemy weakness system to master.

The climactic battle of the demo will reveal Eline’s hidden power, her ability to transform into the Black Beast. This form lets her deal massive damage and perform enemy executions… but at what cost to Eline? She always comes to afterwards with no memories of what just happened.

Edge of Memories is a successor to Midgar’s previous game Edge of Eternity. Where that game was a turn-based JRPG-inspired journey, Edge of Memories adopts a more vibrantly colourful art direction and real-time battles.

“Edge of Eternity is definitely a welcome addition to the JRPG pantheon,” Steve said in our review of Edge of Eternity, “and while it clearly doesn’t have the budget of the genre giants, it is full of heart and character. I certainly enjoyed my time exploring Heryon, despite a few niggles and a crafting system that adds little. If you go into this with an open mind and have an affection for the genre, then you’ll find much to like.”

Source: press release



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/05/29/an-edge-of-memories-demo-is-coming-to-steam-in-mid-june/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: What We Played – Lego Batman, Mina the Hollower & 007: First Light
item.content:encoded:
Cor blimey. Well, that’s another week in the bag, and in the UK it’s been hotter than a PS5’s armpits, even in the more typically frozen wastelands of the north. Still, I’ve only ventured outside a little, plugging away at a bunch of games including Lego Batman, Lumentale, Darksiders: Warmastered Edition and Forza Horizon 6.

Tef has also played a bit of Lego Batman, as well as a bit more of 007 First Light. He says, “Both are great.” So, that’s all you need to know, right? But if he were to elaborate, he’d point to all the incredible nods, winks and jokes in Lego Bats, and the constantly surprising action adventure feel of 007.

While he has been playing more Skull & Bones, most of Jim’s time has been spent with 007 First Light. He says, “As a huge fan of both Hitman and Uncharted, it’s ticking both boxes for me and is easily my favourite game of the year so far.”

Steve has been playing “Super Mario What the Hell is That Ball of Fire in the Sky, UK edition” though I think that’s a joke, because Nintendo DLC isn’t free. He continues, “Whilst hiding from the heat I have played through The Remake of the End of the Greatest RPG of All Time, Necrophosis, which is a huge improvement on The Shore, The Quiet Place: The Road Ahead, which has been perfect for the ungodly heat of the night, and finally Aliens: Infestation after rescuing my CFW DS from my kids.”

That’s not all though! He also checked out the Early Access for classic ARPG, Kromlech, saying, “Going to put a bit more time in before writing it up as it’s super nostalgic for an old-time Piranha Bytes fan like me, but obviously still needs a lot of polish.”

TC played more Resi Requiem and remembered why he doesn’t like Resi games apart from four and five. He explained, “You need this key, but to get that you need this puzzle piece but before that you need a screwdriver but before that you need some cheese to distract the zombie mice. And the things you need are always miles away from where you need them. It’s just very contrived, old fashioned, and dull. Also what is with bullet sponge zombies that soak up headshots? No. Go away Resi and come back when you’ve made it to 2026.”

So, I don’t think he’s enjoyed it quite as much as the rest of us? He does have his own suggestions, “Someone needs to do Scream (the original movie) for Resident Evil. Here are all the tropes and mechanics that always happen, so let’s NOT do them in the expected way and drag it to the modern day. Box with fuse screwed shut? Smash the glass with something rather than hunt for a flipping screwdriver. Jeez. Would Nathan Drake look for a screwdriver? No.”

Laura and Ade and have returned to Diablo 4 and started from the top with new characters. Laura told us, “We chose the barbarian and sorcerer this time which we much prefer over our previous druid and necromancer characters as our screen gets less jam-packed with animals, skeletons and golems! Time to kick some Daughter of Hatred butt!!”

Gamoc has played Rune Dice for review, Crimson Desert, and Guardians of the Galaxy. Talking of the latter, he says it’s “Very good, except the combat is pretty shallow and there is way too much of it, drawing things out unnecessarily.” And we’ll wrap things up with Aran, who has been playing Mina The Hollower for review, though we’ll have to wait for next week to see how he’s got on.

What about you? What have you played?



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/05/29/what-we-played-lego-batman-mina-the-hollower-007-first-light/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
